{"number": "001", "authors": [{"id": 787, "loginId": "lrivera2", "lastName": "Rivera", "firstName": "Lee", "isCreator": false, "emailAddress": "lrivera2@example.edu"}, {"id": 1105, "loginId": "jdoe1", "lastName": "Doe", "firstName": "Jamie", "isCreator": true, "emailAddress": "jdoe1@example.edu"}], "outline": "", "samCode": {"code": "C", "name": "Clearly Occupational"}, "subject": {"id": 70, "code": "ACCTG", "value": "ACCTG", "sequence": 6, "isDefault": false}, "courseId": 14486, "outcomes": [{"name": "At the end of the course, a successful student will be able to complete an accounting cycle for a corporation according to generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP) in the United States of America.", "performance": "75.00", "outcomeLevel": "CSLO"}, {"name": "At the end of the course, a successful student will be able prepare basic financial statements.", "performance": "75.00", "outcomeLevel": "CSLO"}], "offerings": [], "startTerm": "2026 Winter", "department": {"id": 56, "code": "ACCTG", "name": "Accounting", "type": {"id": 5, "description": "Program"}, "parentOrganizationEntity": {"href": "?parent_org_entity_id=288"}}, "internalId": 14486, "objectives": [{"name": "1 - Explain the nature and purpose of generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP) and International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS).", "sequence": 0}, {"name": "2 - Explain and apply the components of the conceptual framework for financial accounting and reporting, including the qualitative characteristics of accounting information, the assumptions underlying accounting, the basic principles of financial accounting, and the constraints and limitations on accounting information.", "sequence": 1}, {"name": "3 - Define and use accounting and business terminology.  ", "sequence": 2}, {"name": "4 - Explain what a system is and how an accounting system is designed to satisfy the needs of specific businesses and users; summarize the purpose of journals and ledgers.", "sequence": 3}, {"name": "5 - Apply transaction analysis, input transactions into the accounting system, process this input, and prepare and interpret the four basic financial statements.  ", "sequence": 4}, {"name": "6 - Distinguish between cash basis and accrual basis accounting and their impact on the financial statements, including the revenue recognition and matching principles.  Identify and illustrate how the principles of internal control are used to manage and control the firms resources and minimize risk.  Explain the content, form, and purpose of the basic financial statements, including footnotes, and the annual report, and how they satisfy the information needs of investors, creditors, and other users.  Explain the nature of current assets and related issues, including the measurement and reporting of cash and cash equivalents, receivables and bad debts, and inventory and cost of goods sold.", "sequence": 5}, {"name": "7 - Identify and illustrate issues relating to long-term asset acquisition, use, cost allocation, and disposal.", "sequence": 6}, {"name": "8 - Distinguish between capital and revenue expenditures.  ", "sequence": 7}, {"name": "9 - Explain the valuation and reporting of current liabilities, estimated liabilities, and other contingencies.  ", "sequence": 8}, {"name": "10 - Identify and illustrate issues relating to long-term liabilities, including issuance, valuation, and retirement of debt, including the time value of money.  ", "sequence": 9}, {"name": "11 - Identify and illustrate issues relating to stockholders equity, including issuance, repurchase of capital stock, and dividends.  ", "sequence": 10}, {"name": "12 - Explain the importance of operating, investing and financing activities reported in the statement of cash flows.  Interpret company activity, profitability, liquidity and solvency through selection and application of appropriate financial analysis tools.  ", "sequence": 11}, {"name": "13 - Identify the ethical implications inherent in financial reporting and be able to apply strategies for addressing them.  ", "sequence": 12}, {"name": "14 - Explain the nature and reporting of income tax payment allocation and payroll tax reporting.  ", "sequence": 13}, {"name": "15 - Final examination.  ", "sequence": 14}], "requisites": {"type": "OR", "blockList": []}, "assignments": "", "courseTitle": "Introductory Accounting I", "naturalKeys": [], "courseStatus": "In Development", "courseTopics": [], "curriculumId": "010508", "customFields": [{"customFieldID": "TBA", "customFieldName": "To Be Arranged (TBA)", "customFieldValue": ["No"]}, {"customFieldID": "TBAHours", "customFieldName": "TBA Hours", "customFieldValue": ["0.0"]}, {"customFieldID": "TBAActivities", "customFieldName": "TBA Activities", "customFieldValue": []}, {"customFieldID": "targetCourseContent", "customFieldName": "Target Course Content", "customFieldValue": []}, {"customFieldID": "COESpecified", "customFieldName": "COE Non-course Condition Type", "customFieldValue": []}, {"customFieldID": "COENoncourseOther", "customFieldName": "COE Non-course Condition - Program: 050200 State ID: 20229"]}, {"customFieldID": "CB10", "customFieldName": "Cooperative Work Experience Education Status (CB10)", "customFieldValue": ["N - is not part of a work experience education program"]}, {"customFieldID": "passingGradeMeetsMathCompetency", "customFieldName": "Passing Grade Meets Math Competency for all AA/AS degrees", "customFieldValue": ["No"]}, {"customFieldID": "lectureContent", "customFieldName": "Course Content", "customFieldValue": ["Global Perspective Highlights: Discuss major similarities between (GAAP) standards in the U.S. and the IASB (115 countries/30 member states). Areas under discussion: Preparing accounts and adjusting financial statements, reporting assets and liabilities and the closing process, accounting for merchandise purchases and sales, income statement presentation and Balance sheet presentation, items and costs making up inventory, assigning costs to inventory and estimating inventory costs, recognition of receivables, valuation and disposition of receivables, accounting for plant and intangible assets, characteristics of liabilities (known and estimated). Purposes and uses of accounting information.   The accounting cycle.   Cash and accrual accounting.   Internal controls.   Financial reporting rules and disclosure, including international standards, such as International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS).  Assets.   Current liabilities.   Long-term liabilities.   Stockholders equity and reporting requirements.   Measuring cash flow.   Financial statements and statement analysis.   Ethical issues.   Business and income tax payments and reporting.   Final examination.  "]}, {"customFieldID": "assignCreativeExpression", "customFieldName": "Creative Expression", "customFieldValue": ["No"]}, {"customFieldID": "assignTeamwork", "customFieldName": "Teamwork and Value Systems", "customFieldValue": ["No"]}, {"customFieldID": "diversityStatementDescription", "customFieldName": "Diversity Statement Description", "customFieldValue": ["Accounting has a world-wide cultural and historical background. The course brings forth to the students the various origins of the mathematical systems and reporting concepts. All cultures have had and have various forms of accounting and reporting."]}, {"customFieldID": "assignDemoInfoComp", "customFieldName": "Information Competency Description", "customFieldValue": These announcements and guides offer a comprehensive review of the assignment topics. Further, lectures or discussions are provided in the topical modules. The students have open access to present questions or their insights. The SLOs and Course Objectives are matched to course content for complete coverage. The assignments offer repeated exposure for competent accomplishment. Further the substantive examinations provide both the student and instructor notice as to their accomplishment--complete feedback is provided to examinations. "DE Accessibility", "customFieldValue": ["Acknowledged: All methods of instruction and evaluation used in DE sections conform to all of the following: 1) Alternative text or alternative descriptions will be provided for all images. 2) Instructional videos will have accurate closed captioning. 3) Transcripts will be provided for all audio recordings. 4) Pages will use structured headings (such as Header 2 for section headings) accessible to a screen reader. 5) Hyperlinks will be presented using meaningful link text rather than URLs. 6) Content will provide adequate color contrast (such as black and white background), font size (such as 12-14 points), and font style (such as Arial or Tahoma) to ensure readability. 7) All PDF files will be text-based, not scanned, and use true headings (such as those created with the Styles menu in MS Word for saving as PDF)."]}, {"customFieldID": "DEUniversalDesign", "customFieldName": "DE Universal Design", "customFieldValue": ["Acknowledged: All methods of instruction and evaluation used in DE sections conform to all of the following: 1) Provide an uncluttered interface with consistent layout and navigation. 2) Avoid moving or flashing images and self-starting video or audio. 3) Ensure access for people with diverse abilities. 4) Accommodate a wide range of individual preferences and abilities. 5) Communicate necessary information to the user regardless of ambient conditions or the user's sensory abilities."]}, {"customFieldID": "DEStrategiesForParticipatoryActivities", "customFieldName": "DE Strategies for Participatory Activities",
